//: skill-runs substrate config: skillRuns.baseDir (DL-001/C-003), skillRuns.retentionDays (DL-005), skillRuns.copyTranscript (DL-030: copy native subagent transcript into the run dir on SubagentStop; default true is behavior-preserving — set false to rely solely on native transcripts via cleanupPeriodDays, a NATIVE-FIRST/DL-015 opt-out), CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS (DL-009), CLAUDE_SKILL_DEBUG_TEAMS=1 (DL-029: opt-in, default off — append raw Teams-hook payloads to skill-runs-debug/team-payloads.jsonl for field verification; bounded by a 50 MB cap). Spawn restriction (DL-015, M-008b): Python dispatch-prose guards removed; spawn surface now constrained by native primitives only — Agent(type) allowlists in each orchestrator/lead definition's tools field; on the leaf side every agents/*.md worker carries disallowedTools:Agent (architect additionally a read-only tools:[Read,Grep,Glob,Bash] allowlist) so a spawned worker cannot itself spawn on either the Agent-tool or Agent Teams path (sub-agents.md:158), guarded by tests/test_static_integrity.py::TestAgentToolConstraints; permissions.deny:["Agent(<name>)"] here for disabled agents; headless: set CLAUDE_AGENT_SDK_DISABLE_BUILTIN_AGENTS=1.

statusLine.//: Run-aware status line: model + cwd, plus a '◇ N active (skill ·M⚡)' segment when the durable substrate has running skill runs. Dependency-free, bounded scan, never errors (skills/scripts/statusline.py). Synced to ~/.claude/skills/scripts/ by sync.sh.

permissions.//: defaultMode 'auto' routes tool calls through the classifier, which already blocks irreversible/destructive actions (e.g. git push --force, git reset --hard) and anything aimed outside the environment. No permissions.deny git rules are added: prefix-matched Bash globs are leaky (miss trailing-flag forms) and redundant with the classifier. Tighten via autoMode.soft_deny prose rules if a specific destructive pattern slips through.

env.//CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS: Enables Agent Teams mode (experimental). When unset/empty, skills degrade gracefully to Workflow tool + Agent-tool subagents with IDENTICAL resume semantics (DL-009). Now committed as '1' to activate teammate coordination (messaging, shared task list, inter-agent debate) for the adversarial skills (decision-critic, deepthink, problem-analysis). This supersedes DL-T1-04, which only warned against committing an EMPTY value (treated as absent by select_orchestration_mode(), so it misrepresented the runtime); a concrete '1' accurately represents enabled mode. To revert to the subagent fallback, set to '0' or remove the key below.
env.//ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_MODELS: Central model provisioning for the custom agents (agents/*.md). Each agent's frontmatter declares a tier alias (opus/sonnet/haiku) rather than a pinned full model ID; these vars pin what each alias resolves to (model-config.md:146). Behavior-preserving: the values below reproduce the IDs the agents pinned before the alias migration (architect=opus-4-8, the sonnet workers=sonnet-4-6, scout=haiku-4-5-20251001). To swap a whole tier in the future, change the one line here — every agent on that alias follows. Tier-granular: agents sharing an alias move together. To pin an individual agent off-tier, put a full model ID back in its frontmatter instead.
